steps.title: Define the challenged claim
steps.content: The underlined claim says Medellin's style is so idiosyncratic that it resists being viewed through nationality or cultural identity. Because the question asks for a challenge, the quotation must push against that idea by saying the work can be classified through one of those cultural lenses, not merely by naming one influence.

steps.title: Separate support from challenge
steps.content: A identifies one Maya influence without classifying the whole work, B supports the idea that different traditions are synthesized, and C says critics overfocus on Indigenous influence while excluding other influences. The remaining quotation goes further by saying the work "invites classification as Indigenous art," directly contradicting the claim that classification by cultural identity is resisted. The answer is D.
